children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, in coordination with the Commandant of the Coast Guard and the Secretary of State, shall establish a subworking group to address IUU fishing in the exclusive economic zone of the United States in the Gulf of Mexico.December 20, 2019

children.label: A
children.text: the surveillance, interdiction, and prosecution of any foreign nationals engaged in such fishing; and

children.label: B
children.text: the application of the provisions of the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act ( et seq.) to any relevant nation, including the status of any past or ongoing consultations and certification procedures;16 U.S.C. 1826d

actions and policies, in addition to the actions and policies described in paragraph (1), each of the Federal agencies described in subsection (a) can take, using existing resources, to combat IUU fishing in the exclusive economic zone of the United States in the Gulf of Mexico; and
any additional authorities that could assist each such agency in more effectively addressing such IUU fishing.
the findings identified pursuant to subsection (b); and
a timeline for each of the Federal agencies described in subsection (a) to implement each action or policy identified pursuant to subsection (b)(2).
